core_mechanism: kinetic Monte Carlo sampling of a multi-stage sequential stochastic (Markov jump) process, where a tunable coupling / co-localization parameter reshapes the distribution of first-passage (completion) times over which the successive stages fire
brief_reason: The method uses Monte Carlo simulation of a multi-step activation cascade to compute how a control parameter (scaffold concentration / spatial localization of components) broadens or narrows the distribution of times at which the sequential steps become activated.
